summary: Silky‑smooth matrix mix captures a stellar Philly ’88 show – Althea, Cold Rain, Women > Eyes and an epic Other One blaze.
review: The Healy‑engineered soundboard/audience matrix is described as crisp, silky and turn‑up‑to‑three‑o‑clock perfect, making the recording feel like you’re right in the hall. Musically the concert is a fan favorite – tight jams, standout transitions (Women into Eyes), soaring Althea and Cold Rain, plus a long, fiery Other One. Overall reviewers are overwhelmingly enthusiastic, noting both superb performance and exceptional audio.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high 4‑5 star reviews praise both the show (standout songs, strong jams) and the recording (Healy mix, crisp matrix), yielding a rating well above average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Healy mix described as silky‑smooth, crisp, balanced soundboard/audience blend; listeners note it can be turned up past three o’clock without distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong first‑set flow with solid Jack Straw and Althea, a seamless Women → Eyes transition, and an expansive post‑space jam on The Other One that keeps the energy high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Tone described as perfect/crisp; solo praised; vocals noted as better than previous shows; his jam at the end of Women highlighted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Shows “good chops” in Eyes and a standout outro jam on The Other One; perked up during post‑space sections.
band_member_comments.Bob: Critiqued for bringing Jack Straw out of the solo a bit early, but overall solid rhythm/vocal work noted.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboards) called “awesome” and praised for singing on Women; keyboards generally lauded throughout the show.
